records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Waylaid (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Waylaying.]

defs: To lie in wait for; to meet or encounter in the way; especially, to watch for the passing of, with a view to seize, rob, or slay; to beset in ambush.
quotes: Falstaff, Bardolph, Peto, and Gadshill shall rob those men that we have already waylaid.
She often contrived to waylay him in his walks.
